text_original: Oh the Necessity.oe Stahdaeds Motse said: To accomplish anjrthing whatsoever one must have standards.
text_normalized: Oh the Necessity.oe Stahdaeds Motse said: To accomplish anjrthing whatsoever one must have standards.

text_original: None has yet accomplished anything without them.
text_normalized: None has yet accomplished anything without them.

text_original: The gentlemen fulfilling their duties as generals and councillors have their standards.
text_normalized: The gentlemen fulfilling their duties as generals and councillors have their standards.

text_original: Even the artisans performing their tasks also have their standards.
text_normalized: Even the artisans performing their tasks also have their standards.

text_original: The artisans make square objects according to the square, circular objects according to the compasses; they draw straight lines with the carpenter’s line and find the perpendicular by a pendulum.
text_normalized: The artisans make square objects according to the square, circular objects according to the compasses; they draw straight lines with the carpenter’s line and find the perpendicular by a pendulum.

text_original: All artisans, whether skilled or unskilled, employ these five standards.
text_normalized: All artisans, whether skilled or unskilled, employ these five standards.

text_original: Only, the skilled workers are accurate.
text_normalized: Only, the skilled workers are accurate.

text_original: Though the unskilled labourers have not attained accuracy, yet they do better by followiag these standards than otherwise.
text_normalized: Though the unskilled labourers have not attained accuracy, yet they do better by followiag these standards than otherwise.

text_original: Thus all artisans follow the standards in their work.
text_normalized: Thus all artisans follow the standards in their work.

text_original: Now, the government of the empire and that of the large states do not observe their standards.
text_normalized: Now, the government of the empire and that of the large states do not observe their standards.

text_original: This shows the governors are even less intelligent than the artisans.
text_normalized: This shows the governors are even less intelligent than the artisans.

text_original: What, then, should be taken as the proper standard m government?
text_normalized: What, then, should be taken as the proper standard m government?

text_original: How will it do for everybody to imitate Will or Hevaen which is somehow here omitted, namely find the level with water Ms parents?
text_normalized: How will it do for everybody to imitate Will or Hevaen which is somehow here omitted, namely find the level with water Ms parents?

text_original: There are numerous parents in the world but few are magnanimous.
text_normalized: There are numerous parents in the world but few are magnanimous.

text_original: For everybody to imitate Ms parents is to imitate the unmagnanimous.
text_normalized: For everybody to imitate Ms parents is to imitate the unmagnanimous.

text_original: Imitating the unmagnanimous can not be said to be following the proper standard.
text_normalized: Imitating the unmagnanimous can not be said to be following the proper standard.

text_original: How will it do for everybody to follow his teacher?
text_normalized: How will it do for everybody to follow his teacher?

text_original: There are numerous teachers in the world but few are magnanimous.
text_normalized: There are numerous teachers in the world but few are magnanimous.

text_original: For everybody to imitate his teacher is to imitate the unmagnanimous.
text_normalized: For everybody to imitate his teacher is to imitate the unmagnanimous.

text_original: Imitating the un-magnanimous cannot be taken as following the proper standard.
text_normalized: Imitating the un-magnanimous cannot be taken as following the proper standard.

text_original: How will it do for everybody to imitate his ruler?
text_normalized: How will it do for everybody to imitate his ruler?

text_original: There are many rulers in the world but few are magnanimous.
text_normalized: There are many rulers in the world but few are magnanimous.

text_original: For everybody to imitate the ruler is to imitate the unmagnanimous.
text_normalized: For everybody to imitate the ruler is to imitate the unmagnanimous.

text_original: Imitating the iinmagnanimous cannot be taken as following the right standard.
text_normalized: Imitating the iinmagnanimous cannot be taken as following the right standard.

text_original: So then neither the parents nor the teacher nor the ruler should be accepted as the standard in government.
text_normalized: So then neither the parents nor the teacher nor the ruler should be accepted as the standard in government.

text_original: What then should be taken as the standard in government?
text_normalized: What then should be taken as the standard in government?

text_original: Nothing better than following Heaven.
text_normalized: Nothing better than following Heaven.

text_original: Heaven is all-inclusive and impartial in its activities, abundant and unceasing in its blessings, and lasting and imtiring in its guidance.
text_normalized: Heaven is all-inclusive and impartial in its activities, abundant and unceasing in its blessings, and lasting and imtiring in its guidance.

text_original: And, so, when the sage-kings had accepted Heaven as their standard, they measured every action and enterprise by Heaven.
text_normalized: And, so, when the sage-kings had accepted Heaven as their standard, they measured every action and enterprise by Heaven.

text_original: W"hat Heaven desired they would carry out, what Heaven abominated they refrained from.
text_normalized: W"hat Heaven desired they would carry out, what Heaven abominated they refrained from.

text_original: Now, what is it that Heaven desires, and what that it abominates?
text_normalized: Now, what is it that Heaven desires, and what that it abominates?

text_original: Certainly Heaven desires to have men benefit and love one another and abominates to have them hate and harm one another.
text_normalized: Certainly Heaven desires to have men benefit and love one another and abominates to have them hate and harm one another.

text_original: How do we know that Heaven desires to have men love and benefit one another and abominates to have them hate and harm one another?
text_normalized: How do we know that Heaven desires to have men love and benefit one another and abominates to have them hate and harm one another?

text_original: Because it lo¥es and benefits men universally.
text_normalized: Because it lo¥es and benefits men universally.

text_original: How do we know that it loves and benefits men universally?
text_normalized: How do we know that it loves and benefits men universally?

text_original: Because it claims all and accepts offerings from all.
text_normalized: Because it claims all and accepts offerings from all.

text_original: All states in the world, large or small, are cities of Heaven, and all people young or old, honourable or humble, are its subjects; for they ail graze oxen and sheep, feed dogs and pigs, and prepare clean wine and cakes to sacrifice to Heaven.
text_normalized: All states in the world, large or small, are cities of Heaven, and all people young or old, honourable or humble, are its subjects; for they ail graze oxen and sheep, feed dogs and pigs, and prepare clean wine and cakes to sacrifice to Heaven.

text_original: Does this not mean that Heaven claims all and accepts offerings from all?
text_normalized: Does this not mean that Heaven claims all and accepts offerings from all?

text_original: Since Heaven does claim all and accepts offerings from all, what then can make us say that it does not desire men to love and benefit one another?
text_normalized: Since Heaven does claim all and accepts offerings from all, what then can make us say that it does not desire men to love and benefit one another?

text_original: Hence those who love and benefit others Heaven will bless.
text_normalized: Hence those who love and benefit others Heaven will bless.

text_original: Those who hate and harm others Heaven will curse, for it is said that he who murders the innocent will be visited by misfortune.
text_normalized: Those who hate and harm others Heaven will curse, for it is said that he who murders the innocent will be visited by misfortune.

text_original: How else can we explain the fact that men, murdering each other, will be cursed by Heaven?
text_normalized: How else can we explain the fact that men, murdering each other, will be cursed by Heaven?

text_original: Thus we are certain that Heaven desires to have men love and benefit one another and abominates to have them hate and harm one another.
text_normalized: Thus we are certain that Heaven desires to have men love and benefit one another and abominates to have them hate and harm one another.

text_original: The ancient sage-kings, Yii, T’ang, Wen, and Wu loved the people of the world universally, leading them to reverence Heaven and worship the spirits.
text_normalized: The ancient sage-kings, Yii, T’ang, Wen, and Wu loved the people of the world universally, leading them to reverence Heaven and worship the spirits.

text_original: Many were their benefits to the people.
text_normalized: Many were their benefits to the people.

text_original: And, thereupon Heaven blessed them, establishing them emperors; and all the feudal lords of the empire showed them respect.
text_normalized: And, thereupon Heaven blessed them, establishing them emperors; and all the feudal lords of the empire showed them respect.

text_original: (On the other hand) the wicked kings, CSbieh, Chow, Yu, and Li, hated all the people in the world, seducing the people to curse Heaven and ridicule the spirits.
text_normalized: (On the other hand) the wicked kings, CSbieh, Chow, Yu, and Li, hated all the people in the world, seducing the people to curse Heaven and ridicule the spirits.

text_original: Great were their injuries to the people.
text_normalized: Great were their injuries to the people.

text_original: Thereupon Heaven brought them calamity, depriving them of their empire and their lives; and posterity condemned them to this day.
text_normalized: Thereupon Heaven brought them calamity, depriving them of their empire and their lives; and posterity condemned them to this day.

text_original: Chieh, Chow, Yu, and Li, then, are those that committed evil and were visited by calamities.
text_normalized: Chieh, Chow, Yu, and Li, then, are those that committed evil and were visited by calamities.

text_original: And, Yii, T’ang, Wen, and Wii are those that loved and benefited the people and obtained blessings.
text_normalized: And, Yii, T’ang, Wen, and Wii are those that loved and benefited the people and obtained blessings.

text_original: Thus we have those who obtained blessings because they loved and benefited the people as well as those who were visited by calamities because they hated and harmed the people.
text_normalized: Thus we have those who obtained blessings because they loved and benefited the people as well as those who were visited by calamities because they hated and harmed the people.
